# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image
FROM node:14

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json
COPY package*.json ./

# Install system utilities and build tools
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    curl \
    git \
    make \
    build-essential \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Node.js dependencies
RUN npm install

# Copy the rest of your application code into the container
COPY . .

# Expose the port the app runs on
EXPOSE 3000

# Command to run the app
CMD ["npm", "start"]
